mission.title: telepresence: sshfs won't work inside WSL on Windows, or inside Docker by default
mission.description: sshfs won't work inside WSL on Windows, or inside Docker by default. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current telepresence setup
steps.description:
Verify your telepresence version and Kubernetes connectivity:
```bash
telepresence version
telepresence status
kubectl get pods -n ambassador
```
This feature requires a working telepresence installation.

steps.title: Review the issue
steps.description: Telepresence uses `sshfs` for volume mounts during intercepts, but `sshfs` does not work inside Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) or inside Docker containers by default. This prevents remote volume mounts from functioning in these environments.

steps.title: Apply the docker-mount workaround
steps.description:
The fix adds support for using the [vieux/sshfs](https://github.com/vieux/docker-volume-sshfs) Docker volume driver instead of `sshfs` directly. A new `--docker-mount` CLI option is added, mutually exclusive with `--mount`.

This works when telepresence is run inside a container. Note that it does not currently work when run from WSL.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions and implementation details.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test the new `--docker-mount` option:
```bash
telepresence intercept <deployment> --docker-mount --port <port>:<port>
```
Confirm that volume mounts work correctly when running telepresence inside a Docker container.

resolution.summary: Added support for using the vieux/sshfs Docker volume driver via a new --docker-mount CLI option as an alternative to native sshfs, enabling volume mounts when running telepresence inside Docker containers.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with telepresence installed. Install via: brew install datawire/blackbird/telepresence (macOS) or binary download.
